findings.title: R1 length matches the expected fixed structure

evidence.note: R1 should be exactly 28 bp (sum of its fixed-length segments)

findings.title: Cell barcodes on the 10x whitelist

evidence.note: R1[0:16] should match the 3M-february-2018 gel-bead barcode whitelist

findings.detail: 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the whitelist

findings.title: R2 reads beginning with the TSO (adapter-dimer / short insert)

evidence.note: R2 should start with cDNA; a leading TSO is the hallmark of empty/short-insert products

findings.detail: 32.6% of R2 start with the TSO

findings.title: R1 read-through into the 3' adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem in R1 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 0.0% of R1 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGCACA

findings.title: R2 read-through into the 3' adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem in R2 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 3.6% of R2 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGCACA

findings.title: R2 with a poly-G no-signal tail

evidence.note: a poly-G 3' tail on two-color instruments is 'no signal' — typical of empty/short fragments

findings.detail: 9.8% of R2 have a poly-G tail

ranked.why: 32.6% of R2 begin with the TSO versus a <5% threshold (6.5x over). Per spec, R2 should start with cDNA; a leading TSO is the direct hallmark of empty/short-insert (adapter-dimer) products, so a third of the library carries no usable insert.

9.8% of R2 carry a poly-G no-signal tail versus a <1% threshold (~10x over). On this two-color Illumina platform poly-G means 'no signal', i.e. the fragment ran out of insert — independent corroboration of the empty/short-fragment population flagged by the TSO check.

3.6% of R2 contain the AGATCGGAAGAGCACA adapter stem versus <2% (mild ~1.8x exceedance), indicating some inserts were shorter than the 91 bp read — consistent with, but a much weaker signal than, the short-insert population above.
R1 is exactly 28 bp (16 bp CB + 12 bp UMI) as the v3/v3.1 fixed structure requires; barcode read geometry is intact.
94.9% of cell barcodes match the 3M-february-2018 whitelist (>85% threshold), so GEM formation and barcode integrity are healthy — the failure is not in cell barcoding.
0.0% R1 adapter read-through; the 28 bp R1 shows no read-through and behaves exactly as specified.

plan.root_cause: A large fraction of the library consists of empty / short-insert products (TSO-only, no-cDNA molecules), most consistent with low cDNA input or poor reverse-transcription / template yield during library prep rather than any barcoding or sequencing-geometry fault.
plan.diagnosis: About a third of your R2 reads start with the TSO (32.6% vs a <5% expectation) and ~10% end in a poly-G no-signal tail, both signatures of empty/short-insert fragments with little or no cDNA — meanwhile R1 length (28 bp) and barcode whitelist match (94.9%) are healthy, so GEM formation and barcoding are fine. This points to a cDNA/insert problem, most likely low RNA input or weak reverse-transcription yield producing adapter-dimer–like TSO artifacts. Check cDNA yield/traces and input quantity, and consider a size-selection cleanup before re-sequencing, since a substantial share of your reads carry no usable transcript.
